species: human gut microbiota (genus-abundance-weighted average of 22 cultured commensal species; e.g. Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron, Phocaeicola vulgatus, Agathobacter/Eubacterium rectale, Faecalibacterium prausnitzii, Bifidobacterium, Roseburia, Blautia, Prevotella copri, Ruminococcus bromii, Escherichia coli

growth_records.strain: mixed (22 type/reference strains from repositories; per key resources table)

up.units: mmol glucose-equiv / g biomass (per-biomass yield demand, community-weighted)
up.method: reported (HPLC per-biomass uptake, abundance-weighted average across genera)

sec.method: reported (HPLC per-biomass excretion, abundance-weighted average across genera)

yields.note: Yxs approx = 1/13 = 0.077 g biomass per mmol glucose equivalents; >90% of MAC carbon ends in fermentation products

growth_records.cite: Arnoldini et al. Quantifying the varying harvest of fermentation products from the human gut microbiota. Cell (2025). DOI:10.1016/j.cell.2025.07.005. [PMC12556654]
growth_records.snippet: Using the measured amount of carbohydrate needed to produce bacterial biomass (on average 13 mmol glucose equivalents/g biomass) ... a daily fermentation product release of 16 g * 29 mmol/g ~ 450 mmol
growth_records.notes: Only community/abundance-weighted per-biomass values are numerically stated in the full text: carbohydrate demand = 13 mmol glucose-equiv per g biomass; total fermentation product excretion = 29 mmol per g biomass (YCA medium). Yxs = 1/13 mmol = 0.077 g biomass per mmol glucose. Per-species growth rates (mu, Fig 1G exponential rates by linear regression on log-OD) and per-species qS/qP (Fig 1D/1E) exist but their numeric values reside only in figures and Data S1 (external supplement), NOT in the machine-readable XML, so they are not transcribed here to avoid fabrication. Grew 22 species also in BHIS, epsilon and gamma defined media across pH 5.5-7.5; per-biomass uptake/excretion nearly constant across media/pH though growth rates vary.
